Details: GAC adsorption was somewhat effective for the removal of the following acetochlor degradates: ESA, OXA, hydroxyacetochlor, deschloroacetochlor, 2-chloro-2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide, 2-hydroxy-2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide, and 2'-ethyl-6'-methylacetanilide.  Based on data available from two full-scale studies, GAC typically achieved between 27 and 52 percent removal when employed concurrently with conventional treatment. Activated carbon treatment was found to be more effective for acetochlor than for its degradates.
